graphdb_rdf.metric_question: Under what circumstances does an AI system developed for military purposes fall back within the scope of this Regulation?
graphdb_rdf.metric_answer: An AI system developed for military purposes falls within the scope of the EU AI Act under specific circumstances. According to the regulation, AI systems that are primarily designed for military applications are generally considered out of the scope of this regulation. However, they may fall under its purview if they are used for purposes that are not strictly military or if they intersect with civilian applications. The regulation outlines that while AI systems can be used for military purposes, they are subject to regulatory relations and usage relations defined in the context of the Act. This means that if a military AI system is utilized in a manner that impacts civilian life or public safety, it may become subject to the obligations and rights established by the EU AI Act. For further details, you can refer to the entity related to "military purposes" in the regulation: [Military Purposes](https://euaiact.eu/resource/Activity/military_purposes).
